name: Anise Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, beat butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Beat in eggs one at a time until batter is smooth.
3. Add vanilla, anise, lemon zest, and juice, flour, baking powder, and salt. Mix just until thoroughly blended.
4. Mix in walnuts. Cover and chill dough for at least 4 hours.
5. Divide dough into several portions.
6. On a lightly floured board, roll each portion of dough into logs about 1 1/2 inches in diameter and the length of the baking sheet.
7. Bake logs, spaced 2 to 3 inches apart, in a pre-heated oven set to 325°F for 30 minutes, or until golden and firm to the touch.
8. Remove from oven and let cool for 10 minutes.
9. Lower oven temperature to 300°F.
10. Cut logs into 3/4-inch thick slices. Place slices, cut side down, on baking sheets.
11. Bake for 15 minutes, until biscotti are dry.
12. Cool on wire racks.
13. Makes 8 dozen.
